title: In A Breton Cemetery
content:
They sleep well here,
    These fisher-folk who passed their anxious days
    In fierce Atlantic ways;
    And found not there,
    Beneath the long curled wave,
    So quiet a grave.

    And they sleep well
    These peasant-folk, who told their lives away,
    From day to market-day,
    As one should tell,
    With patient industry,
    Some sad old rosary.

    And now night falls,
    Me, tempest-tost, and driven from pillar to post,
    A poor worn ghost,
    This quiet pasture calls;
    And dear dead people with pale hands
    Beckon me to their lands.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Ernest Christopher Dowson delivers a powerful performance in "In A Breton Cemetery"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
